2604.25850.title: Agentic Harness Engineering: Observability-Driven Automatic Evolution of Coding-Agent Harnesses
2604.25850.paper_summary: Harnesses have become a central determinant of coding-agent performance, shaping how models interact with repositories, tools, and execution environments. Yet automating harness engineering is hard: a heterogeneous action space, sparse and noisy evaluation signal, multi-million-token trajectories, and edits whose effect is hard to attribute to the next round's outcomes. We introduce Agentic Harness Engineering (AHE), a framework that automates harness-level evolution by instrumenting the three stages of any engineering loop (component editing, trajectory inspection, and decision making) with matched observability pillars: (1) component observability gives every editable harness component a file-level representation so the action space is explicit and revertible; (2) experience observability distills millions of raw trajectory tokens into a layered, drill-down evidence corpus that an evolving agent can actually consume; and (3) decision observability pairs every edit with a self-declared prediction, later verified against the next round's task-level outcomes. Together, these pillars turn every edit into a falsifiable contract, so harness evolution proceeds autonomously without collapsing into trial-and-error. Empirically, ten AHE iterations lift pass@1 on Terminal-Bench 2 from 69.7% to 77.0%, surpassing the human-designed harness Codex-CLI (71.9%) and the self-evolving baselines ACE and TF-GRPO. The frozen harness transfers without re-evolution: on SWE-bench-verified it tops aggregate success at 12% fewer tokens than the seed, and on Terminal-Bench 2 it yields +5.1 to +10.1pp cross-family gains across three alternate model families, indicating the evolved components encode general engineering experience rather than benchmark-specific tuning. These results position observability-driven evolution as a practical pathway to keep coding-agent harnesses continually improving.
2604.25850.paper_summary_zh: 鞍具已成為編碼代理性能的核心決定因素，塑造模型如何與儲存庫、工具和執行環境互動。<lb/>然而，自動化鞍具工程是困難的：異質的行動空間、稀疏且嘈雜的評估信號、數百萬標記的軌跡，以及其效果難以歸因於下一輪結果的編輯。<lb/>我們介紹了代理鞍具工程（AHE），這是一個通過為任何工程循環的三個階段（組件編輯、軌跡檢查和決策制定）配備匹配的可觀察性支柱來自動化鞍具級演變的框架：<lb/>(1) 組件可觀察性為每個可編輯的鞍具組件提供文件級別的表示，使得行動空間變得明確且可還原；<lb/>(2) 經驗可觀察性將數百萬個原始軌跡標記提煉成一個分層的、深入的證據語料庫，讓不斷演變的代理實際上可以消耗；<lb/>(3) 決策可觀察性將每個編輯與自我聲明的預測配對，並在後續驗證與下一輪的任務級結果。<lb/>這些支柱共同將每個編輯轉變為可反駁的契約，因此鞍具演變自動進行，而不會陷入試錯中。<lb/>實證上，十次AHE迭代將Terminal-Bench 2的pass@1從69.7%提升至77.0%，超越了人類設計的鞍具Codex-CLI（71.9%）以及自我演變的基準ACE和TF-GRPO。<lb/>凍結的鞍具在不重新演變的情況下轉移：在SWE-bench驗證中，它在比種子少12%的標記下達到最高的總成功率，而在Terminal-Bench 2中，它在三個替代模型系列中產生+5.1至+10.1pp的跨系列增益，表明演變的組件編碼了通用的工程經驗，而不是基準特定的調整。<lb/>這些結果將以可觀察性驅動的演變定位為持續改進編碼代理鞍具的實用途徑。
2604.25850.author: Jiahang Lin et.al.
2604.25850.authors: Jiahang Lin, Shichun Liu, Chengjun Pan, Lizhi Lin, Shihan Dou, Xuanjing Huang, Hang Yan, Zhenhua Han, Tao Gui
